steps.title: Spot the agreement signal
steps.content: The word "concurring" is the key signal—it indicates that Rehren is supporting the same conclusion about glass availability that Hodgkinson reached.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is addition/similarity — Hodgkinson reached a conclusion about glass availability, and Rehren concurred with the same conclusion. The blank needs a transition signaling parallel agreement.
  • Choice A — "As such,": signals a consequence or conclusion drawn from what preceded it, so fails — Rehren's concurrence is not caused by Hodgkinson's finding. ✗
  • Choice B — "Likewise,": signals that a second party does or concludes the same thing, so fits. ✓
  • Choice C — "Nonetheless,": signals contrast (despite X, Y still holds), so fails — Rehren agrees with Hodgkinson rather than opposing him. ✗
  • Choice D — "Consequently,": signals a direct result of what preceded it, so fails — Rehren's agreement is parallel, not a consequence of Hodgkinson's work. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
